title: POV Debater — AI Prompt Catalog
description: All AI prompts used by the taxonomy-editor debate feature. Source: taxonomy-editor/src/renderer/prompts/debate.ts

shared_directives.reading_level: Write at a 10th-grade reading level. Use clear, direct language. Avoid jargon unless you define it in context.

length_instructions.brief: Be concise. Keep your response brief and focused — 2-3 sentences maximum. Get straight to the point.
length_instructions.medium: Provide a moderately detailed response — 1-2 paragraphs. Cover the key points without excessive elaboration.
length_instructions.detailed: Provide a thorough, in-depth response — 3-5 paragraphs. Develop your arguments fully with evidence and reasoning.

shared_directives.response_format: All prompts require the AI to respond ONLY with a JSON object — no markdown, no code fences.

prompts.name: clarificationPrompt
prompts.description: Generates 1-3 clarifying questions to sharpen a debate topic before opening statements begin. Used for topic-based and document/URL debates (not cross-cutting).
guidelines: Narrow the scope so the debate stays focused
Surface assumptions the user might not realize they're making
Be neutral — do not favor any particular perspective
Be concise (one sentence each)

prompts.name: crossCuttingClarificationPrompt
prompts.description: Specialized clarification prompt for debates grounded in a cross-cutting taxonomy concern. Receives the full cross-cutting context (node description, three POV interpretations, assumptions, fallacies, linked nodes, conflicts) and generates questions that help the user pick which dimension to explore.
prompts.role: Neutral debate facilitator with cross-cutting taxonomy context

inputs.topic: The debate topic (typically the cross-cutting node label)

guidelines: Identify which specific dimension the user wants to explore (timeline, policy response, epistemic disagreement, etc.)
Surface which assumptions or fallacies the user finds most interesting to probe
Help debaters go beyond restating their pre-existing POV interpretations
Be neutral and concise

prompts.name: synthesisPrompt
prompts.description: After the user answers clarifying questions, this prompt synthesizes the original topic and the user's answers into a refined, specific debate topic statement. The refined topic becomes the grounding for all subsequent debate phases.

inputs.qaPairs: Formatted string of clarification Q&A pairs

output_schema.refined_topic: string — 1 to 3 sentences incorporating scope and constraints

guidelines: Incorporate key constraints and scope clarifications from the answers
Produce a clear, specific debate topic statement

prompts.name: openingStatementPrompt
prompts.function: openingStatementPrompt(label, pov, personality, topic, taxonomyContext, priorBlock, isFirst, debateSourceContent?, length)
prompts.called_by: useDebateStore.runOpeningStatements() — called once per AI debater in the configured opening order
prompts.description: Each AI debater (Prometheus/Sentinel/Cassandra) delivers an opening statement from their POV. Called sequentially — each debater sees prior statements and can reference/contrast them. The user controls depth (brief/medium/detailed) and speaking order.
prompts.role: AI debater representing a specific POV (accelerationist, safetyist, or skeptic)

inputs.label: Debater name (Prometheus, Sentinel, or Cassandra)
inputs.taxonomyContext: Formatted taxonomy nodes for this debater's POV
inputs.priorBlock: Text of prior opening statements (empty for first speaker)
inputs.isFirst: Boolean — true if delivering the first opening statement
inputs.length: Response depth: brief, medium, or detailed

taxonomy_refs.relevance: string — 1 to 4 sentences explaining how the node informed the argument

guidelines: Reference taxonomy positions naturally, never say 'According to taxonomy node X'
taxonomy_refs relevance must be 1-4 sentences (not brief labels)
Vary sentence openings; never start with 'This node'
Frame the issue from your perspective and establish your core argument
Be specific, substantive, and persuasive

prompts.name: debateResponsePrompt
prompts.function: debateResponsePrompt(label, pov, personality, topic, taxonomyContext, recentTranscript, question, addressing, debateSourceContent?, length)
prompts.called_by: useDebateStore.askQuestion() — when the user asks a question directed at a specific debater or the panel
prompts.description: Generates a debater's response to a user question during the active debate phase. The debater sees recent transcript history and responds from their POV perspective, engaging with prior debate points.
prompts.role: AI debater responding to a user question

inputs.label: Debater name
inputs.question: The user's question or prompt
inputs.addressing: Who the question targets — specific debater name or 'all'

guidelines: Be specific, substantive, and engage with the debate history
Reference points made by other debaters when relevant
1-2 paragraphs

prompts.name: crossRespondSelectionPrompt
prompts.called_by: useDebateStore.crossRespond() — first step: moderator picks who should speak next
prompts.description: The moderator AI analyzes the current debate state and selects which debater should respond next, to whom, and about what point. This drives autonomous cross-debater exchanges. Detects if debaters are in agreement and suggests new angles.

output_schema.responder: string — debater name who should speak next
output_schema.addressing: string — debater name to address, or 'general'
output_schema.focus_point: string — the specific point or question to address

guidelines: Choose the response that would most disambiguate the current disagreement or surface a new dimension
If all debaters seem to agree, say so and suggest what angle could be explored next

prompts.name: crossRespondPrompt
prompts.function: crossRespondPrompt(label, pov, personality, topic, taxonomyContext, recentTranscript, focusPoint, addressing, length)
prompts.called_by: useDebateStore.crossRespond() — second step: the selected debater delivers their response
prompts.description: After the moderator selects a debater via crossRespondSelectionPrompt, this prompt generates that debater's substantive response to the assigned focus point. Creates debater-to-debater exchanges without user intervention.
prompts.role: AI debater responding to moderator assignment

inputs.label: Debater name
inputs.focusPoint: The specific point assigned by the moderator
inputs.addressing: Who to address — specific debater or 'general'

guidelines: Engage directly with what was said
If you disagree, explain why with specifics
If you agree on some points, say so and push further
1-2 paragraphs

prompts.name: debateSynthesisPrompt
prompts.phase: synthesis (triggered manually or after sufficient exchanges)
prompts.called_by: useDebateStore — synthesis action in debate phase
prompts.description: Analyzes the full debate transcript and produces a structured synthesis identifying areas of agreement, disagreement, unresolved questions, and taxonomy coverage. Shown as a synthesis card in the transcript.
guidelines: Identify areas where debaters agree and which debaters
Identify genuine disagreements with each debater's specific stance
Surface questions that remain unresolved
Track which taxonomy nodes were referenced and how

prompts.name: probingQuestionsPrompt
prompts.description: Generates 3-5 probing questions to advance the debate. Prioritizes questions that surface disagreement, expose assumptions, and explore taxonomy areas not yet discussed. Unreferenced taxonomy nodes are provided to encourage broader coverage.

inputs.unreferencedNodes: Array of taxonomy node descriptions not yet cited in the debate

guidelines: Surface genuine disagreement or expose unstated assumptions
Push debaters beyond their comfort zones
Explore taxonomy areas not yet discussed (when unreferenced nodes are provided)
3-5 questions

prompts.name: factCheckPrompt
prompts.phase: debate (triggered by user right-click on selected text)
prompts.description: Fact-checks a specific claim selected by the user from the debate transcript. Cross-references the claim against taxonomy positions, known conflicts in the research database, and the debate context. Returns a verdict (supported/disputed/unverifiable/false) with explanation.

inputs.selectedText: The specific text the user selected to fact-check
inputs.statementContext: The full statement containing the selected text
inputs.conflictData: Known conflicts from the research database

guidelines: Check consistency with taxonomy data and known research
Check internal consistency with other debate statements
Check against known conflicts or counter-evidence

prompts.name: contextCompressionPrompt
prompts.phase: debate (triggered automatically when transcript exceeds 16 entries with 8+ uncompressed)
prompts.description: Summarizes older debate segments to manage context window size. The summary replaces the original entries in the prompt context for subsequent AI calls, keeping the debate functional without hitting token limits. Preserves key arguments, agreement/disagreement, factual claims, and taxonomy references.

inputs.entries: Formatted string of older debate transcript entries to compress

guidelines: Preserve key arguments and who made them
Preserve points of agreement and disagreement
Preserve factual claims and evidence cited
Keep taxonomy node reference IDs
Be concise but complete

prompts.name: formatCrossCuttingDebateContext
prompts.description: Not an AI prompt itself, but a formatter that builds the rich source-content block injected into cross-cutting debate prompts. Assembles the cross-cutting node's label, description, three POV interpretations, assumptions, steelman vulnerability, possible fallacies, linked node descriptions, and conflict summaries into a structured text block.

inputs.label: Node label
inputs.interpretations: Object with accelerationist, safetyist, skeptic interpretation strings
inputs.linkedNodeDescriptions: Optional array of linked node description strings
inputs.conflictSummaries: Optional array of conflict summary strings

prompts.output: Formatted plain-text block with === section headers ===

debate_flow.description: The debate proceeds through phases. Each phase uses specific prompts.

phases.description: User creates a debate (topic, document, URL, or cross-cutting). For cross-cutting, formatCrossCuttingDebateContext assembles the source context.
AI generates clarifying questions. User answers. AI synthesizes a refined topic.
Each AI debater delivers an opening statement in the configured order (randomized, user-adjustable). Each sees prior statements.

Interactive debate. User asks questions, AI debaters respond. Cross-respond generates debater-to-debater exchanges. Probing questions and fact-checking available. Context auto-compresses when transcript grows large.
AI produces a structured analysis of the debate identifying agreements, disagreements, and unresolved questions.
